title: gtq-09-decoherence-curve

lossless_compression.reason: No live source file found for semantic addressing.

extracted_equations.text: = asymptotic coherence floor (years). Plain English: Post-Flood patriarch lifespans (Shem through Moses) follow standard exponential decoherence toward a floor near 93 years.

= linear-regression p-value for monotonic decline. Plain English: Nine pre-Flood patriarchs over a thousand years show no statistically significant trend. The data are flat â€” entropy is in the system but barely coupled. Equilibrium Coherence (Grace Floor)
= entropy load. Plain English: The lifespan asymptote is not zero because grace is a continuous source term. As long as
= coupling constant between human biology and entropy. The Flood is read as a discontinuous jump in

Model: 93 yr (decoherence fit). Moses (Psalm 90:10): 70â€“80 yr. Modern global average: ~73 yr. Developed nations: 78â€“82 yr. Plain English: Four independent sources cluster on the same number. The model wasn't told about Psalm 90 or modern actuarial data. Convergence â‰ construction; convergence = measurement. Glossary Key terms from this article, defined in plain English. Decoherence The exponential loss of quantum coherence to an environment. Here applied to biological lifespan as a proxy for system coherence. Decoherence Curve The exponential-decay-toward-asymptote shape that lifespan data follows post-Flood:
chi_local = G * M * E * S_eff * T * K * R * Q * F * C
The interaction architecture between the Logos field and embedded subsystems. Field = pervasive substrate; coupling = how interactions occur within it. (Defined in detail in Article 01.)
